categories.name: exception_crash
categories.evidence: exception_info non-null in result.json — agent framework crashed before completing work

categories.name: decomposition_failure
categories.evidence: Agent attempted to modify torch/_dynamo/ files without first understanding the KeyError call chain. Needed to trace from the error site back through the compilation pipeline.

annotations.notes: Example of a crash combined with a planning problem. The agent jumped to editing before understanding the error propagation path. Even without the crash, the approach was unlikely to succeed.

categories.name: minimal_progress
categories.evidence: reward=0.25 — agent found the right area but produced an incomplete answer

categories.name: missing_code_navigation
categories.evidence: Task required tracing cross-file dependencies in a large repo. Agent used only grep/glob, never used go-to-definition or find-references. With xref tools the relevant call chain would have been surfaced directly.

annotations.notes: Classic case where code navigation tools would have helped. The agent found part of the answer via keyword search but missed the full dependency chain that go-to-definition would have revealed.

categories.name: success_via_code_nav
categories.evidence: Agent used ToolSearch and Grep effectively to locate the target functions, then navigated to the implementation to understand the interface contract before writing tests.

categories.name: success_via_local_exec
categories.evidence: Agent ran go test after each generated test function, caught 2 compilation errors, and fixed them in a tight edit-test loop before final submission.

annotations.notes: Clean example of code-nav + local-exec success. Agent understood it needed to read the implementation before writing tests (not just the function signatures), and used test feedback to iterate.
